description: Wire the Command Centre data pipeline end-to-end: connect proactive agents to write items, schedule enrichment + prioritisation crons, mount frontend realtime/deep-links/keyboard hooks, fix snooze, and clean up 200KB+ of dead code.

userStories.title: Wire morning briefing agent to Command Centre
userStories.description: Add writeToCommandCentre() call to agent-morning-briefing so each user's daily briefing creates a CC item with source_agent='morning-brief', item_type='insight'.
acceptanceCriteria: Import writeToCommandCentre from _shared/commandCentre/writeAdapter.ts
Call writeToCommandCentre after the insert_agent_activity RPC (~line 95) with org_id from persona.org_id, user_id from persona.user_id
Set item_type='insight', source_agent='morning-brief', urgency='normal'
Title includes deal/meeting counts from briefing data
Summary contains first 500 chars of narrativeBriefing
Wrapped in try-catch so CC failure never breaks briefing delivery
Deploy to staging and verify item appears in command_centre_items table
userStories.notes: Pattern: follow agent-crm-heartbeat/index.ts:196 which already writes to CC. persona loop gives per-user items.

userStories.title: Wire pipeline analysis agent to Command Centre
userStories.description: Replace or supplement the existing createActionCentreItems() call in proactive-pipeline-analysis with writeMultipleItems() targeting command_centre_items. Map severity→urgency, use existing summary.insights array.
acceptanceCriteria: Import writeMultipleItems from _shared/commandCentre/writeAdapter.ts
At ~line 559, replace or supplement createActionCentreItems() with writeMultipleItems()
Map each insight to CC item: source_agent='pipeline-analysis', item_type from insight.actionType
Add mapSeverityToUrgency helper: critical→critical, high→high, medium→normal, low→low
Include deal_id and contact_id from insight data where available
Wrapped in try-catch so CC failure never breaks pipeline analysis
Deploy to staging, run pipeline analysis manually, verify items appear in command_centre_items
userStories.notes: Existing summary.insights array (lines 351-495) has all the data. Consider keeping old Action Centre call temporarily for backwards compat.

userStories.title: Wire meeting prep agent to Command Centre
userStories.description: Add writeToCommandCentre() call to proactive-meeting-prep after log_copilot_engagement RPC, creating a 'meeting_prep' item with urgency='high' and due_date set to meeting start time.
acceptanceCriteria: Import writeToCommandCentre from _shared/commandCentre/writeAdapter.ts
Call writeToCommandCentre after log_copilot_engagement RPC (~line 596)
Set item_type='meeting_prep', source_agent='meeting-prep', urgency='high'
Set due_date to meeting.start_time (prep needed before meeting)
Title: 'Prep: {meeting.title}', summary from prepResult.brief (first 500 chars)
org_id from orgId || prepResult.organizationId, user_id from userId
Wrapped in try-catch so CC failure never breaks meeting prep delivery
userStories.notes: deal_id not directly available at insertion point — enrichment pipeline will resolve from meeting context.

userStories.title: Wire post-meeting agent to Command Centre
userStories.description: Add writeMultipleItems() call to slack-post-meeting after action items are extracted from the debrief, creating 'follow_up' items with urgency='high'.
acceptanceCriteria: Read full slack-post-meeting/index.ts to find exact action item extraction point
Import writeMultipleItems from _shared/commandCentre/writeAdapter.ts
Map each extracted action item to a CC item: source_agent='post-meeting', item_type='follow_up'
Set urgency='high', include deal_id from meeting.deal.id where available
Include meeting context (meeting_id, meeting_title) in the context JSONB field
Wrapped in try-catch so CC failure never breaks post-meeting flow
Deploy to staging and verify items appear after a test meeting ends
userStories.notes: Need to read full file first to locate extraction point. Use writeMultipleItems for batch efficiency.

userStories.title: Create cron schedule for cc-enrich (every 15 min)
userStories.description: Create a new Supabase migration that schedules cc-enrich via pg_cron every 15 minutes. This is the CRITICAL missing piece — without it, items stay at enrichment_status='pending' forever and never get drafted actions.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration file in supabase/migrations/ following naming convention
pg_cron schedule: */15 * * * * calling cc-enrich edge function via net.http_post
Pass CRON_SECRET or service_role_key in Authorization header (follow cc-auto-execute pattern from 20260222700005)
Body includes { source: 'cron' } to distinguish from manual invocations
Apply migration to staging environment (ref: caerqjzvuerejfrdtygb)
Verify in Supabase dashboard that cron job appears and fires
Check cc-enrich logs show processing within 15 minutes of deployment
userStories.notes: Follow exact pattern from 20260222700005_schedule_cc_auto_execute_cron.sql. Fleet-health already expects 15-min cadence. cc-enrich handles batch (20 items/run), rate limits, and semaphore internally.

userStories.title: Create cron schedule for cc-prioritise (every 10 min)
userStories.description: Create a new migration scheduling cc-prioritise via pg_cron every 10 minutes, offset by 3 minutes from the enrichment cycle to avoid overlap.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration file in supabase/migrations/ following naming convention
pg_cron schedule: 3,13,23,33,43,53 * * * * (offset from enrichment cycle)
Pass service_role_key in Authorization header
Body includes { batch: true, source: 'cron' } for batch mode
Apply migration to staging
Verify items get priority_score and urgency values after cron fires
Verify DEDUP-003 merge group recheck triggers on items with multiple agents
userStories.notes: Runs in batch mode to score all open/enriching/ready items. Also triggers DEDUP-003 auto-approve if merged confidence >= 0.7.

userStories.title: End-to-end enrichment pipeline verification
userStories.description: Verify the full item lifecycle on staging: insert test item → cc-enrich picks it up → actionDrafter generates drafted_action → cc-prioritise scores it → status becomes 'ready'. Debug and fix any issues found.
acceptanceCriteria: Insert test CC item via SQL with source_agent='manual-test', item_type='follow_up'
Within 15 min: enrichment_status changes from 'pending' to 'enriched'
drafted_action field is populated (not null) with type, payload, display_text
confidence_score is set (0.0-1.0 range)
Within 25 min: priority_score and urgency are set by cc-prioritise
status progresses to 'ready'
Item appears in the Command Centre UI under the 'Needs You' filter
If AI enrichment fails: check ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / model config, verify actionDrafter fallback path
userStories.notes: This is a verification story, not a code change. May require debugging cc-enrich logs, checking API keys, or fixing actionDrafter. Critical gate before frontend work matters.

userStories.title: Mount realtime hook in Command Centre page
userStories.description: Add useCommandCentreRealtime() call to CommandCentre.tsx so items added by agents appear in the feed without manual refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: Import useCommandCentreRealtime from @/lib/hooks/useCommandCentreItemsQuery
Call useCommandCentreRealtime() at the top of the CommandCentre component (before state declarations)
Verify: insert a CC item via SQL while the page is open → item appears without pressing Refresh
Verify: approve an item in another tab → status updates in the current tab
Realtime publication already enabled in migration 20260225000005 (no DB changes needed)
userStories.notes: 1-line addition. useRealtimeHub is working-hours aware (full during 8AM-6PM, minimal off-hours). Table-side realtime already configured.

userStories.title: Wire deep links for bookmarkable Command Centre URLs
userStories.description: Connect the existing useCommandCentreDeepLinks hook to CommandCentre.tsx so URLs like /command-centre?item={id}&filter=needs-you work.
acceptanceCriteria: Import useCommandCentreDeepLinks from @/lib/hooks/useCommandCentreDeepLinks
Call the hook with { items: allItems, onSelectItem: setDetailItem, onSelectFilter: setActiveFilter }
When user opens detail panel: URL updates to include ?item={id}
When user changes filter: URL updates to include ?filter={name}
When user closes detail panel: ?item param is removed from URL
Loading /command-centre?item={id} directly opens the detail panel for that item
Loading /command-centre?filter=deals applies the Deals filter on mount
userStories.notes: Hook is fully implemented — just needs 3 wiring points: init, panel open/close, filter change.

userStories.title: Wire keyboard navigation for Command Centre
userStories.description: Connect the existing useCommandCentreKeyboard hook to CommandCentre.tsx for j/k/Enter/a/d/Esc navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Import useCommandCentreKeyboard from @/lib/hooks/useCommandCentreKeyboard
Call the hook with { items: filteredItems, selectedItem: detailItem, onSelectItem, onApprove, onDismiss, isPanelOpen }
j/k keys move highlight through items in the feed
Enter toggles the detail panel for the highlighted item
a key approves the highlighted item (only if status is open/ready)
d key dismisses the highlighted item
Escape closes the detail panel
Keyboard shortcuts are disabled when focus is in an input/textarea/contenteditable
userStories.notes: May need to add isHighlighted prop to CCItemCard for visual highlight state.

userStories.title: Fix snooze to hide items from Command Centre feed
userStories.description: Add a due_date filter to commandCentreItemsService.getItems() so snoozed items (with due_date pushed into the future) don't appear in the feed until their snooze expires.
acceptanceCriteria: In getItems() query (commandCentreItemsService.ts ~line 104-153), add filter: or('due_date.is.null,due_date.lte.now()')
Snooze an item → it immediately disappears from the feed
After snooze time expires → item reappears in the feed
Items with no due_date continue to appear normally
Items with a past due_date continue to appear normally
userStories.notes: Single-line query addition. Alternative: add a snoozed_until column (cleaner but needs migration). Start with the filter approach.

userStories.title: Delete orphaned command-centre components and demo pages
userStories.description: Remove 21 orphaned component files from src/components/command-centre/, the unused useCommandCentreTasks hook, and 3 demo pages (200KB+ of hardcoded mock data). Update commandCentreStore.ts to remove dependency on deleted types.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete entire src/components/command-centre/ directory (21 files, all verified orphaned)
Delete src/lib/hooks/useCommandCentreTasks.ts (unused, queries wrong table)
Update src/lib/stores/commandCentreStore.ts to remove import from deleted types.ts (inline the types or import from commandCentreItemsService)
Delete src/pages/platform/CommandCentreDemo.tsx (45KB hardcoded mock data)
Delete src/pages/platform/CommandCentreV2Demo.tsx (85KB hardcoded mock data)
Delete src/pages/platform/CommandCentreWowDemo.tsx (70KB hardcoded mock data)
Remove any route config entries for deleted demo pages
Build passes with no import errors (npm run build)
userStories.notes: Do last — no functional impact but large diff. Run build after deletion to catch any hidden imports. ~200KB+ removed.
